info.title: NetSuite REST Record API
info.description: NetSuite REST Record API generated 2026-04-16 at 08:46:51 UTC for account 4815401, user nathaniel.talag@ubix.com.ph with role WordPress API Role.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The search query that is used to filter results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The limit used to specify the number of results on a single page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The offset used for selecting a specific page of results.

parameters.name: expandRecords
parameters.description: Set this parameter with value 'true' to enable batch operations for GET requests.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: A comma separated list of internal or external IDs. Prefix external IDs with 'eid:'. Only used for batch operations.

parameters.name: Prefer
parameters.description: The server behavior requested by the client. Use 'respond-async' to execute the request asynchronously. If the request is executed asynchronously, 'Preference-applied: respond-async' is returned in the response.

parameters.name: X-NetSuite-Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A user-defined unique idempotency key that is applied to every asynchronous requests to ensure that the request is executed only once. Only one request can be executed with every unique idempotency key. Use UUID in string format as defined by RFC 4122. If the request is executed synchronously, this value is ignored.

Preference-Applied.description: The server behavior requested by the client. Use 'respond-async' to execute the request asynchronously. If the request is executed asynchronously, 'Preference-applied: respond-async' is returned in the response.

Location.description: A URL to the newly created asynchronous job.

parameters.name: X-NetSuite-PropertyValueValidation
parameters.description: Sets the strictness of property value validation.

parameters.name: X-NetSuite-PropertyNameValidation
parameters.description: Sets the strictness of property name validation.

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: The names of sublists on this record. All sublist lines will be replaced with lines specified in the request. The names are delimited by comma.

parameters.name: replaceSelectedFields
parameters.description: If set to 'true', all fields that should be deleted in the update request, including body fields, must be included in the 'replace' query parameter.

parameters.name: Prefer
parameters.description: The server behavior requested by the client. Use 'respond-async' to execute the request asynchronously. If the request is executed asynchronously, 'Preference-applied: respond-async' is returned in the response.

Location.description: A URL to the newly created asynchronous job.

zip.title: Zip
zip.description: Enter the postal code the way it should appear on all forms except checks.

addr2.title: Address 2
addr2.description: Enter an optional second address line the way it should appear on forms. For employees, customers, partners, and vendors, what you enter here autofills on forms if this address is marked default for Shipping or Billing. Enter up to 50 characters.

city.title: City
city.description: Enter the city the way it should appear on all forms except checks.

addr1.title: Address 1
addr1.description: Enter the address the way it should appear on forms. For employees, customers, partners, and vendors, what you enter here autofills on forms if this address is marked default for Shipping or Billing. Enter up to 50 characters. This field is required for the Online Bill Pay feature.

addressee.title: Addressee
addressee.description: Enter the name of the entity that should appear on the shipping label here. This name appears on the shipping label below what you enter in the Attention field.

attention.title: Attention
attention.description: Enter the name of the person to whom a shipment is addressed, as it should appear on shipping labels. This field is required for UPS Integration.

override.title: Override
override.description: Check this box to disable the free-form address text field. When this field is disabled, text entered in the other address fields does not display in the Address text field. Clear this box to allow text entered in the address component fields to appear in the free-form address text field.

state.title: State/Province
state.description: Enter your company's state or province the way it should appear on all forms except checks.

addrText.title: Address
addrText.description: The values entered in the other address fields are displayed here.

type.title: Type
type.description: A URI reference to the documentation about the problem type.

title.title: Title
title.description: A human-readable description of the problem type.

status.title: Status
status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the server the request originates from.

o:errorDetails.description: An array containing one or more problem types.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: A detailed, human-readable description of the problem occurrence.

o:errorPath.title: Error Path
o:errorPath.description: The JSON path that indicates where the problem occurs within the request body.

o:errorUrl.title: Error URL
o:errorUrl.description: The URI of the first element in the request URL where the problem occurs.

o:errorHeader.title: Error Header
o:errorHeader.description: The name of the HTTP header where the problem occurs.

o:errorQueryParam.title: Error Query Parameter
o:errorQueryParam.description: The name of the query parameter where the problem occurs.

o:errorCode.title: Error Code
o:errorCode.description: The application-specific error code. Similar problem types are grouped together.

zip.title: Zip
zip.description: Enter the postal code the way it should appear on all forms except checks.

addr2.title: Address 2
addr2.description: Enter an optional second address line the way it should appear on forms. For employees, customers, partners, and vendors, what you enter here autofills on forms if this address is marked default for Shipping or Billing. Enter up to 50 characters.

city.title: City
city.description: Enter the city the way it should appear on all forms except checks.

addr1.title: Address 1
addr1.description: Enter the address the way it should appear on forms. For employees, customers, partners, and vendors, what you enter here autofills on forms if this address is marked default for Shipping or Billing. Enter up to 50 characters. This field is required for the Online Bill Pay feature.

addressee.title: Addressee
addressee.description: Enter the name of the entity that should appear on the shipping label here. This name appears on the shipping label below what you enter in the Attention field.

attention.title: Attention
attention.description: Enter the name of the person to whom a shipment is addressed, as it should appear on shipping labels. This field is required for UPS Integration.

override.title: Override
override.description: Check this box to disable the free-form address text field. When this field is disabled, text entered in the other address fields does not display in the Address text field. Clear this box to allow text entered in the address component fields to appear in the free-form address text field.

state.title: State/Province
state.description: Enter your company's state or province the way it should appear on all forms except checks.

addrText.title: Address
addrText.description: The values entered in the other address fields are displayed here.

itemDescription.title: Description
itemDescription.description: A more complete Description of the item.

unit.title: Units
unit.description: If the assembly uses Units of Measure, the base units are displayed in the Units field.

quantity.title: Quantity
quantity.description: In the Quantity to Build field, enter the number of assembly items you want to build. You cannot enter a quantity that exceeds the amount displayed in the Buildable Quantity field.

itemDescription.title: Description
itemDescription.description: A more complete Description of the item.

unit.title: Units
unit.description: If the assembly uses Units of Measure, the base units are displayed in the Units field.

quantity.title: Quantity
quantity.description: In the Quantity to Build field, enter the number of assembly items you want to build. You cannot enter a quantity that exceeds the amount displayed in the Buildable Quantity field.

items.title: Items
items.description: An array field that represents a collection of elements, for example, sublist lines, multiselect items, or search results.

itemCostTaxRate1.title: Tax Rate
itemCostTaxRate1.description: NetSuite inserts the GST rate based on the tax code you assigned to this item. To set up tax codes and corresponding tax percentages, go to Lists > Tax Codes. To change the assignment of a tax code to an item, go to Lists > Items.

subtotal.title: Subtotal
subtotal.description: This field shows the total of the items on this invoice. This is the total before any discounts, shipping cost, handling cost or tax is added to the order.

amountPaid.title: Amount Paid
amountPaid.description: Displays the total amount paid for this invoice.

userEstimatedPaymentDate.title: User-Estimated Payment Date
userEstimatedPaymentDate.description: Enter your internal estimate for when payment is expected, if known. This estimate may be based on your company's own projection or on a payment date that your customer has committed to. This field is optional. Note: If you enter a date, you must also select a value in User-Estimated Payment Date Source.

timeDiscPrint.title: Print
timeDiscPrint.description: Check this box if you added a discount or markup and want to print it on the invoice.

custbody_sii_not_reported_in_time.title: Billing Record Cannot Be Submitted On Time

timeDiscAmount.title: Amount
timeDiscAmount.description: The rate you enter automatically appears here. If you entered a percentage in the Rate field, NetSuite multiplies the discount or markup percentage by the total amount of line items you select.

shipAddress.title: Ship To
shipAddress.description: The default shipping address autofills this field from the customer's record. To enter a different address: * Select another address in the Ship To Select field. * Select New in the Ship To Select field to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record. * Select Custom in the Ship To Select field to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record). * Click the Edit icon for the Ship To Select field to edit an existing shipping address.

toBePrinted.title: To Be Printed
toBePrinted.description: When processing individual invoices: Check the To Be Printed box if you want to print this invoice. When bulk processing invoices: * Select Yes if you want to save this in a queue of invoices to print. * Select No if you do not want to print this form. * Select Respect Customer Preference to send the form based on the default preference on the customer record. Set the preference on the Info subtab of a customer record in the Send Transactions Via field.

itemCostDiscTaxable.title: Taxable
itemCostDiscTaxable.description: Check this box if you want the discount or markup you entered to be applied before taxes.

toBeEmailed.title: To Be Emailed
toBeEmailed.description: When processing individual invoices: * Check the To Be Emailed box if you want to email this invoice. Then enter the email address in the space to the right of the check box. * You can enter multiple email addresses by separating them with a semicolon. It is important to separate the addresses with only a semicolon, and do not include any spaces. When bulk processing invoices: In the To Be Emailed field: * Select Yes if you want to email this form. * Select No if you do not want to email this form. * Select Respect Customer Preference to send the form based on the default preference on the customer record. Set the preference on the Info subtab of a customer record in the Send Transactions Via field.

discountDate.title: Discount Date
discountDate.description: The date listed here is the date your customer must pay this invoice by in order to receive a discount. The discount your customer receives in based on the terms you have set for the customer.

fob.title: FOB
fob.description: Enter the location where the customers technically acquire ownership. For example, if your customers own goods as soon as they ship and pay the shipping costs from your location to theirs, you might enter Our warehouse.

exchangeRate.title: Exchange Rate
exchangeRate.description: The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field. You can edit the exchange rate for this transaction only, or you can update the currency record with the exchange rate you enter here.

toBeFaxed.title: To Be Faxed
toBeFaxed.description: When processing individual invoices: * Check the To Be Faxed box if you want to fax this invoice. Then enter the fax number in the box to the right of the check box if it does not already appear. When bulk processing invoices: In the To Be Faxed field: * Select Yes if you want to fax this form. * Select No if you do not want to fax this form. * Select Respect Customer Preference to send the form based on the default preference on the customer record. Set the preference on the Info subtab of a customer record in the Send Transactions Via field. To fax NetSuite forms, an administrator must first set up fax service at Setup > Company > Printing, Fax and Email Preferences.

message.title: Customer Message
message.description: The text of the customer message you select in the Select Message field appears here. You can edit the message or enter a new customer message for only this transaction.

amountRemainingTotalBox.title: Amount Remaining
amountRemainingTotalBox.description: The total unpaid amount on this invoice is shown here.

dueDate.title: Due Date
dueDate.description: Type or pick the due date for this invoice. If you do not assign a due date, the due date defaults to the date in the Date field. In addition, if you do not assign a due date, this invoice will appear on aging reports.

tranDate.title: Date
tranDate.description: NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this invoice. You can type or pick another date. What you enter here determines the date range in which this transaction appears on the Accounts Receivable Register. You can select or enter custom date ranges on registers.

memo.title: Memo
memo.description: Enter an optional memo to describe this invoice so you can easily distinguish it from others for this customer. For example: Suite A installation or Suite B installation. It will appear on reports such as the 2-line Accounts Receivable register, which your customers can see if you give them permission to log in and view their transaction history.

timeDiscRate.title: Rate
timeDiscRate.description: The rate for the discount or markup item you selected automatically appears here. If you want, enter a new rate. Rates can be entered as dollar amounts and percentages.

estGrossProfitPercent.title: Est. Gross Profit Percent
estGrossProfitPercent.description: Estimated Gross Profit Margin, as a percentage: Read-only field that equals the Est. Gross Profit divided by revenue, expressed as a percentage. At the transaction level, it equals the gross profit percent of all lines, factoring transaction-level discounts and markups.

discountAmount.title: Discount Amount
discountAmount.description: The amount listed here is the discount your customer will receive if he or she pays this invoice according to the terms you have set.

timeTaxRate1.title: Tax Rate
timeTaxRate1.description: NetSuite inserts the GST rate based on the tax code you assigned to this time. To set up tax codes and corresponding tax percentages, go to Lists > Tax Codes. To change the assignment of a tax code to an item, go to Lists > Items.

amountRemaining.title: Amount Remaining
amountRemaining.description: Displays the total amount due for this invoice.

discountRate.title: Rate
discountRate.description: NetSuite enters the rate for the discount item you selected. You can change the discount rate for this cash refund. Enter the discount as a dollar amount like 10.00, or as a percentage like 10%.

custbody_doc_num_summ_invoice.title: Last Document Number Of Summary Invoice

isTaxable.title: Taxable
isTaxable.description: Place a check mark in this box if the order you are invoicing is taxable.

expCostTaxRate1.title: Tax Rate
expCostTaxRate1.description: NetSuite inserts the GST rate based on the tax code you assigned to this expense. To set up tax codes and corresponding tax percentages, go to Lists > Tax Codes. To change the assignment of a tax code to an item, go to Lists > Items.

total.title: Total
total.description: NetSuite computes the total of line items, tax and shipping costs, and then enters it here.

tranId.title: Invoice #
tranId.description: NetSuite increases the largest invoice number by one. You can type another number. The next invoice number will revert to the standard pattern.

expCostDiscAmount.title: Amount
expCostDiscAmount.description: The rate you enter automatically appears here. If you entered a percentage in the Rate field, NetSuite multiplies the discount or markup percentage by the total amount of line items you select.

startDate.title: Start Date
startDate.description: If you use the Advanced Billing feature, enter the date for the first invoice to be created. If an invoice is created from a sales order that includes a Start Date and End Date, those dates autofill on the invoice when the order is billed.

totalCostEstimate.title: Est. Cost
totalCostEstimate.description: Estimated Cost: Estimated cost of the specific number of items; estimated rate x quantity = estimated cost.

timeDiscTaxable.title: Taxable
timeDiscTaxable.description: Check this box if you want the discount or markup you entered to be applied before taxes.

salesEffectiveDate.title: Sales Effective Date
salesEffectiveDate.description: You can change the sales effective date for this transaction. The sales effective date determines which commission plan and historical sales team this transaction applies to.

itemCostDiscPrint.title: Print
itemCostDiscPrint.description: Check this box if you added a discount or markup and want to print it on the invoice.

tax2Total.title: PST
tax2Total.description: NetSuite multiplies the tax rate by the taxable total of line items and enters it here.

trackingNumbers.title: Tracking Numbers
trackingNumbers.description: Enter the shipment tracking number from the shipping company. If a tracking number is entered in the exact format used by UPS or FedEx and the transaction is saved, the tracking number will show on the transaction as a link to the UPS or FedEx website. You can click the link to check the status of the shipment.

custbody_ubix_so_mrt_cmbndttlamtchrgns.title: COMBINED TOTAL AMOUNT TO BE CHARGED (NON-STORE)

taxTotal.title: Tax Total
taxTotal.description: NetSuite multiplies the tax rate by the taxable total of line items and enters it here.

shipDate.title: Ship Date
shipDate.description: Type or pick a shipping date for this invoice.

expCostDiscTaxable.title: Taxable
expCostDiscTaxable.description: Check this box if the expenses you are billing are taxable.

itemCostDiscRate.title: Rate
itemCostDiscRate.description: The rate for the discount or markup item you selected automatically appears here. If you want, enter a new rate. Rates can be entered as dollar amounts and percentages.

itemCostDiscAmount.title: Amount
itemCostDiscAmount.description: The rate you enter automatically appears here. If you entered a percentage in the Rate field, NetSuite multiplies the discount or markup percentage by the total amount of line items you select.

billAddress.title: Bill To
billAddress.description: The default billing address autofills this field from the customer's record. To enter a different address: * Select another address in the Bill To Select field. * Select New in the Bill To Select field to enter a new billing address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record. * Select Custom in the Bill To Select field to enter a new billing address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record). * Click the Edit icon for the Bill To Select field to modify an existing billing address.

estGrossProfit.title: Est. Gross Profit
estGrossProfit.description: Estimated Gross Profit: Read-only field that equals the revenue amount minus the Est. Cost. At the transaction level, it equals the gross profit of all lines, factoring transaction-level discounts and markups.

endDate.title: End Date
endDate.description: Type or pick the end date for this invoice. If an invoice is created from a sales order that includes a Start Date and End Date, those dates autofill on the invoice when the order is billed.

expCostDiscRate.title: Rate
expCostDiscRate.description: The rate for the discount or markup item you selected automatically appears here. If you want, enter a new rate. Rates can be entered as dollar amounts and percentages.

discountTotal.title: Discount Total
discountTotal.description: NetSuite enters the amount discounted on this invoice. If this discount item is taxable, the discount is applied before taxes. If it is not taxable, the discount is applied after taxes.

taxRate.title: Tax Rate
taxRate.description: NetSuite inserts the percentage rate of the tax item from its record at Lists > Items. You can change it here for just this transaction if you wish.

custbody_ubix_so_mrt_combndttlamtchrgd.title: COMBINED TOTAL AMOUNT TO BE CHARGED

otherRefNum.title: PO/Check Number
otherRefNum.description: To make it easy for your customers to locate this invoice in their own records, enter their purchase order number here.

expCostDiscPrint.title: Print
expCostDiscPrint.description: Check this box if you added a discount or markup and want to print it on the invoice.
